articles.title: US Senate unanimously adopts resolution opposing clemency for SBF
articles.summary: The Senate approved a resolution opposing clemency for former FTX CEO Sam Bankman-Fried as prediction markets put the odds of a Trump pardon by July 31 below 1%.

articles.title: Lummis, Gallego secure unanimous Senate approval opposing Sam Bankman-Fried clemency
articles.summary: The U.S. Senate unanimously approved a resolution opposing a presidential pardon or commutation for convicted FTX founder Sam Bankman-Fried.

articles.title: US Senate Unanimously Opposes Sam Bankman-Fried Pardon
articles.summary: The nonbinding resolution said the disgraced FTX founder should 'under no circumstances' receive clemency.

articles.title: Visa says stablecoins will power micro-commerce in AI agentic economy
articles.summary: Visa said it expects agentic commerce to integrate a hybrid flow that combines both card and stablecoin rails at different stages of a task.

articles.title: Tether invests $20M into Argentine neobank Ualá
articles.summary: Tether reportedly invested $20 million into Ualá, as part of the Argentine neobank’s $197 million funding round announced earlier in March.

articles.title: Bitcoin $107K buyers providing ‘early signals’ of 2026 bear-market bottom: Glassnode
articles.summary: Bitcoin realized losses appeared to be copying a reversal structure that marked previous bear-market bottoms, with $69,000 a new BTC price battleground.

articles.title: Robinhood Chain memecoin launchpad Vlad.fun halts after ‘internal integrity’ issue
articles.summary: Vlad.fun suspended its platform after discovering a “serious internal integrity issue” involving members of its team, without disclosing the nature of the alleged misconduct.

articles.summary: A bitcoin OG wallet transferred 5,908 BTC to a new address on Wednesday, according to Lookonchain, citing Arkham data.

articles.title: From Perpetual Swaps to Perpetual CFDs: The Regulated Evolution
articles.summary: The 24/7 Trading Imperative Fixed exchange sessions are a structural mismatch with the global retail base. The NYSE opens at 9:30 a.m. Eastern and closes at 4:00 p.m., a window designed for an era when the participant base was concentrated in a single geography and order flow was physically intermediated. articles.title: Stripe-PayPal deal could accelerate shift to blockchain-based money, Polygon exec says
articles.summary: "Within the next few years the majority of money will live and move on blockchain in one form or another," according to Polygon Labs.

articles.title: Why Analysts Aren’t Worried About Coinbase’s 30% Drop
articles.summary: William Blair slashed earnings estimates by 34% and held its Outperform call. But analysts think Bitcoin's chart may already be answering the bigger question.

articles.title: Public Hyperliquid treasury firm Hyperion enters into new 500k HYPE bond agreement with Skew
articles.summary: Hyperion will deploy 500,000 tokens to Skew through a HYPE Asset Use Service (HAUS) agreement in exchange for equity and revenue share.

articles.title: Strategy CEO says company isn’t going anywhere as bitcoin buyer, sees debt concerns only below $10,000 BTC
articles.summary: Strategy would begin considering risks associated with its debt only if bitcoin falls to around $8,000-$10,000, CEO Phong Le said.

articles.title: Bitcoin to $40,000? If History’s Anything to Go By, It’s Possible, Says Report
articles.summary:
Bitcoin Magazine

Bitcoin to $40,000? If History’s Anything to Go By, It’s Possible, Says Report
Bitcoin could drop as low as $38,000 this year, a new report claims.
This post Bitcoin to $40,000? If History’s Anything to Go By, It’s Possible, Says Report first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Mathew Di Salvo.

articles.title: OpenAI Uses AI Red Team to Strengthen GPT-5.6 Against Prompt Injection Attacks
articles.summary: OpenAI said its new automated red-teaming model, GPT-Red, uncovered vulnerabilities that were used to make GPT-5.6 more resistant to prompt injection attacks.

articles.title: Coinbase's Jesse Pollak steps back from Base app leadership after admitting his crypto social strategy failed
Stanford study says 5-minute Bitcoin prediction markets enable settlement manipulation
articles.summary: Researchers found that Polymarket’s five-minute Bitcoin prediction markets create incentives to manipulate spot prices around contract settlement, proposing longer settlement windows as a potential fix.

articles.title: ‘Hugely positive’ meeting on Thursday with Trump to discuss ethics raises hopes for passage of sweeping crypto legislation
articles.summary: Ethics will be front and center Thursday at a meeting with President Donald Trump, a small group of lawmakers and White House staff.

articles.summary: PrismML's Bonsai 27B runs full reasoning AI on an iPhone for free. We tested it, and it's as impressive as it sounds.

articles.title: Benchmark says Securitize investors should ‘strip out the noise’ after post-SPAC selloff
articles.summary: Separately, Securitize announced a partnership with Cantor Fitzgerald to support blockchain-based IPOs and secondary offerings.

articles.title: What Is BIP-110 and Why Is It Dividing the Bitcoin Community?
articles.summary: A proposal to restrict non-financial data on Bitcoin has split prominent developers, miners, and industry leaders ahead of a key activation deadline, reopening one of the network's biggest governance debates since the Blocksize Wars.

articles.title: Revolut receives in-principle approval from UAE authorities for crypto services
articles.summary: Dubai’s Virtual Assets Regulatory Authority approved the London-headquartered fintech company for broker-dealer, management and investment, and exchange services in the UAE.

articles.title: Base Creator Jesse Pollak Steps Back From App Leadership After Admitting Social Bet 'Was Wrong'
articles.summary: Pollak said he is handing the Base App back to Coinbase to focus on building Base into "the blockchain for global finance" after conceding the network's push into on-chain social missed the mark.

articles.title: BlackRock, Goldman, and JP Morgan Will Give Tokenized Stocks a Try
articles.summary: The Depository Trust & Clearing Corporation is working with nearly 40 financial firms in a pilot to test tokenized stocks and U.S. treasuries.

articles.summary: Coinbase executive Jesse Pollak will focus on Base blockchain's infrastructure, prioritizing trading, stablecoin payments, and AI agents.

articles.summary: President Donald Trump’s pick for attorney general faced criticism from members of the Senate Judiciary Committee on his approach to crypto enforcement and the pardon of former Binance CEO Changpeng Zhao.

articles.title: President Trump To Meet Senators On Clarity Act’s Ethics Fight: Report
articles.summary:
Bitcoin Magazine

President Trump To Meet Senators On Clarity Act’s Ethics Fight: Report
President Trump will meet senators as negotiations over the CLARITY Act's ethics provisions threaten the bill's chances of passing before the August recess.
This post President Trump To Meet Senators On Clarity Act’s Ethics Fight: Report first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

articles.title: Traders Took $8.2 Million From Polymarket’s Five-Minute Bitcoin Bets, Study Found
articles.summary:
Bitcoin Magazine

Traders Took $8.2 Million From Polymarket’s Five-Minute Bitcoin Bets, Study Found
A new study found that Polymarket's five-minute Bitcoin contract functioned as a wealth transfer mechanism, funneling money from retail traders to a small group of manipulators while distorting Bitcoin's spot price.
This post Traders Took $8.2 Million From Polymarket’s Five-Minute Bitcoin Bets, Study Found first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

articles.title: Ostium pauses trading as security firms report multimillion-dollar oracle exploit
articles.summary: Ostium halted trading and advised revoking contract approvals after blockchain security firms reported an apparent oracle-related exploit of its OLP liquidity vault, with estimated losses ranging from $18 million to $22 million.

articles.title: DTCC moves tokenized securities into live trading, marking a milestone for Wall Street's blockchain push
Another DeFi Exploit: Perp DEX Ostium Loses $18 Million in Oracle Attack
articles.summary: Hackers manipulated Ostium's price feed by compromising an oracle signer key, allowing them to drain roughly $18 million from the Arbitrum-based perpetuals exchange.

articles.title: BlackRock outlines vision for crypto-TradFi convergence as product pipeline grows
articles.summary: The firm seeks to offer tokenized long-term investments like "Treasury funds, iShares ETFs, and even private markets."

articles.title: US Treasury Freezes $131 Million in Iran-Linked Crypto Wallets
articles.summary: OFAC sanctioned addresses tied to Iran's central bank and armed forces, with Tether locking four Tron wallets as Washington's financial campaign against Tehran accelerates.

articles.summary: Glacis aims to expand ZeroDelta, its multichain clearing platform for stablecoins, into tokenized securities and foreign exchange over time.

Bitcoin Magazine

Bitcoin price jumped over $65,000 per coin after US inflation data was cooler than expected.

articles.title: Aave launches V4 on Avalanche, laying groundwork for tokenized credit markets
articles.summary: The deployment marks Aave V4’s first expansion beyond Ethereum and introduces lending infrastructure designed to support future markets for tokenized real-world assets.
